authority_order: repeated compatible behavior across independent EasyChart root episodes
SMC/ICT causal mechanics used only to close unresolved gaps
legacy GitHub code only as a falsifiable comparison target
central_thesis: A tradable setup must connect a pre-existing draw on liquidity, a completed liquidity event, displacement that owns a meaningful structure break, a return to the array created by that same displacement, an invalidation level, and the next unconsumed structural objective.

sequence: valid HTF location at external liquidity
close back through the swept level
event-owned displacement through a pre-existing swing
fresh OB/FVG/overlap created by that displacement
exit at nearest pre-existing unconsumed structural objective
event-owned displacement through a pre-existing swing
first retest of the owned FVG/OB/broken boundary
no later opposing sweep superseding the narrative
exit at nearest pre-existing unconsumed structural objective

stop.principle: Place the stop where the causal thesis is invalid, never at an arbitrary fixed distance.
stop.reversal: beyond the sweep extreme plus execution buffer
stop.continuation: beyond the displacement origin or protected swing that owns the break
stop.reentry: beyond the still-valid authority formation extreme

target.selection: nearest valid obstacle in the trade direction
eligible_kinds_in_priority_order: FVG only when separately paired with a liquidity owner

management.at_or_above_1p4_net_r: half at 1R, move remainder stop to breakeven after the partial fill, runner to first obstacle

global_tie_break_after_all_boolean_gates: higher net R to nearest obstacle

blind_proxy_evidence.note: These results belong to the frozen three-family proxy and do not yet prove the structural-target implementation.
